author: linyueqian
body: [Med] This flips streaming params to FINAL_ONLY for every deploy with `async_chunk=false`, which is the default (`OmniModelConfig.async_chunk = False`), so it is not just Qwen3-TTS: any streaming request against an async_chunk=false deploy (moss_tts_nano, voxcpm2, higgs_audio_v2, indextts2, qwen2_5_omni, aura_omni, and others) now gets FINAL_ONLY instead of DELTA coercion. I verified Qwen3-TTS itself streams correctly with `--no-async-chunk` after this change (byte-count parity with the non-streamed run). But if any other async_chunk=false model currently delivers incremental audio under DELTA coercion, this silently turns its streaming into all-audio-at-the-end, a TTFB regression with no error. Did you check the blast radius beyond Qwen3-TTS? If not, gating on `self._tts_model_type == "qwen3_tts"` would match the established convention in this file and keep the change scoped to the model you validated.

author: linyueqian
body: [Low] `AsyncOmni.model_config` returns None when `vllm_config` is None (async_omni.py), and this getattr then silently resolves to True, i.e. back to DELTA coercion. That fails soft (pre-PR behavior), so just a robustness note: reading the resolved deploy config value directly would avoid the silent fallback.

author: linyueqian
body: [Nit] After this change the `seq_len` parameter of `_build_omni_model_runner_output_from_snapshot` has no remaining uses inside the function; the caller still computes `seq_len = hidden_states.shape[0]` and passes it. Consider dropping the parameter (or adding a comment on why it stays) so the next reader does not have to work out which of the two lengths is authoritative.

author: amy-why-3459
body: This appears to be a model-specific variable? Is it possible to move it inside the model?

author: Sy0307
body: Addressed by ede3dddf: the FINAL_ONLY coercion is now scoped to qwen3_tts full-payload serving, and the non-qwen3 async_chunk=false streaming case is pinned by test_prepare_speech_generation_no_async_chunk_stream_keeps_delta_for_non_qwen3. I also reran the targeted H20 test set at head 88dd97bc: 18 passed, 18 warnings in 3.12s (task 391e9cac).

author: Sy0307
body: Addressed in df8137e8: this now reads async_chunk from self.model_config instead of self.engine_client.model_config, so it uses the resolved serving model config and avoids the silent AsyncOmni.model_config None fallback path. Verified in the H20 targeted test set at head 88dd97bc: 18 passed, 18 warnings in 3.12s (task 391e9cac).

author: Sy0307
body: Addressed in df8137e8: removed the dead seq_len parameter from _build_omni_model_runner_output_from_snapshot, removed the caller-side seq_len argument, and cleaned up the affected unit-test call sites. The remaining seq_len passed to _build_omni_pooler_payload is the authoritative mm_seq_len derived from scheduler_output.total_num_scheduled_tokens. Verified in the H20 targeted test set at head 88dd97bc: 18 passed, 18 warnings in 3.12s (task 391e9cac).

author: Sy0307
body: I kept this in serving rather than moving it into the model because the branch controls OpenAI serving message-type coercion (DELTA vs FINAL_ONLY), not model forward behavior. The model-specific part is now narrowly gated to qwen3_tts full-payload serving, with the non-qwen3 async_chunk=false streaming behavior pinned by test_prepare_speech_generation_no_async_chunk_stream_keeps_delta_for_non_qwen3. Verified in the H20 targeted test set at head 88dd97bc: 18 passed, 18 warnings in 3.12s (task 391e9cac).
